Djokovic has won 101 ATP Tour–level singles titles, including a record 24 majors (including a record ten Australian Open titles), a record 40 Masters, a record seven year-end championships, and an Olympic gold medal.
He stepped down in 2020 to front a new player-only tennis association, the Professional Tennis Players Association (PTPA), which he co-founded with Vasek Pospisil, citing the need for players to have more influence on the tour and advocating better prize money structure for lower-ranked players.
2019: Wimbledon and 7th Australian Open titles
2023: Record-breaking 24th major & 7th ATP Finals titles
2025: 100th ATP title and further records broken
Djokovic took a last minute entry to the 2025 Geneva Open, where he defeated Hubert Hurkacz in the final to claim the 100th ATP singles title of his career, becoming the first ever tennis player to win at least one ATP singles title in twenty consecutive seasons, and the 3rd tennis player in the Open Era to have won 100 or more career titles (after Jimmy Connors and Federer).
The pair served as co-presidents of the new organization to promote the interests of male and female tennis players above a ranking of 500 in singles and 200 in doubles, and wishing to give players more influence on tour.
Grand Slam tournament finals: 38 (24 titles, 14 runner-ups)

For the 2024 season, the points breakdown was adjusted to award more points in ATP Tour events.
The ATP Race is an annual performance-based points race to determine the qualifiers for the year-end championship, in addition to the year-end No.
Since a new team competition United Cup was introduced, its participants are also eligible to receive ATP ranking points for won matches (up to 500 points for the entire tournament).
Within the lowest two tiers, points awarded varied based on the prize money of each tournament.

Después de casi cuatro horas de juego, el italiano derrotó a **Alexander Zverev** por 6-7 (7), 7-6 (2), 6-3 y 6-4. De esta manera, revalidó los puntos obtenidos en la edición de la temporada pasada, aunque de todos modos, el **ranking ATP** sufrirá varios cambios a partir de su próxima actualización. ## Sinner le remontó a Zverev, renovó su reinado como bicampeón de Wimbledon y levantó su quinto Grand Slam. ## Roger Federer y un llamado a la nostalgia: volvió a jugar al tenis en Wimbledon. En el segundo lugar quedó **Zverev**, quien tras la ausencia por lesión de **Carlos Alcaraz** y sus buenos desempeños en **Roland Garros** (campeón) y **Wimbledon** (finalista) superó el puntaje del español. ## Así quedó el ranking ATP luego de la final de Wimbledon.","score":0.6479992,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://tennishead.net/novak-djokovic-and-alex-de-minaurs-new-atp-rankings-confirmed-after-their-wimbledon-exits","title":"Novak Djokovic and Alex de Minaur's new ATP rankings confirmed after their Wimbledon exits","content":"# Novak Djokovic and Alex de Minaur’s new ATP rankings confirmed after their Wimbledon exits. **Novak Djokovic and Alex de Minaur were unable to capture the Wimbledon title this year.**. Despite failing to reach the quarter-finals at Wimbledon this year, Djokovic’s defeat at Wimbledon has confirmed a breakthrough for De Minaur. ## Alex de Minaur confirmed to reach career-high after Novak Djokovic’s loss. De Minaur was at a career-high ranking of world number six coming into Wimbledon this year, where he also lost in the fourth round last year. However, following Djokovic’s defeat, De Minaur is now confirmed to reach his career-high ranking when the rankings are updated after Wimbledon.
